rulings.Case Title: PAUL RATNER et al VS. 1314 POLK STREET ASSOCIATES, LLC et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 4, 2009, line 6, DEFENDANT IAN BIRCHALL DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to fourth, eighth, and ninth causes of action. Plaintiff must plead facts that show a unity of interest and ownership between the corporations and defendant Birchall exists to support alter ego allegation. Insufficient facts plead to show defendant Birchall's involvement to support these causes of action. Demurrer overruled as to tenth cause of action for Constructive Trust. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL RATNER et al VS. 1314 POLK STREET ASSOCIATES, LLC et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 4, 2009, line 6, DEFENDANT IAN BIRCHALL MOTION TO STRIKE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Motion to strike granted with ten days leave to amend to allege defendant Birchall's oppression, fraud or malice to support punitive damages. Plaintiff to specify in the prayer which entities and/or individuals are liable for attorney fees. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ZELKO SIMONI et al VS. SIAVASH TAHBAZOF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Zelko Simoni And Renee Simoni'S Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction (Per Ex Parte Order Of 10/19/09)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 4, 2009, line 9, Plaintiffs Zelko Simoni and Renee Simoni's Order to Show Cause re: preliminary injunction. Preliminary injunction granted. Plaintiffs establish imminent and irreparable harm since construction will destroy the current easements. Plaintiff is likely to prevail on the merits with evidence of an easement by implication. Former owners state everyone used the easements and all owners had keys. The balance of equities and harms weigh in plaintiffs' favor. Without an injunction the status quo will change. It does not appear that anything substative is happening on the construction project. Plaintiffs to post a bond in the amount of $10,000 pursuant to CCP section 529. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: TENDERLION HOUSING CLINIC VS. TRENT RHORER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Otion For Issuance Of Peremptory Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 4, 2009, line 11, PETITIONER TENDERLION HOUSING CLINIC Motion For Issuance Of Peremptory Writ Of Mandate. Motion denied. No showing that the City's action in awarding the grant contract to Mary Elizabeth Inn (MEI) was arbitrary or capricious. Substantial evidence support's the City's decision. MEI's lease constitutes a master lease, and MEI has been subleasing the building for more than three years. =(302/CWW)
